root_text.an4.100:1.1: Atha kho potaliyo paribbājako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an4.100:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho potaliyaṁ paribbājakaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an4.100:2.3: Idha, potaliya, ekacco puggalo avaṇṇārahassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena, no ca kho vaṇṇārahassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena.
root_text.an4.100:2.4: Idha pana, potaliya, ekacco puggalo vaṇṇārahassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena, no ca kho avaṇṇārahassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena.
root_text.an4.100:2.5: Idha pana, potaliya, ekacco puggalo neva avaṇṇārahassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena, no ca vaṇṇārahassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena.
root_text.an4.100:2.6: Idha pana, potaliya, ekacco puggalo avaṇṇārahassa ca avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena, vaṇṇārahassa ca vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena.
root_text.an4.100:2.7: Ime kho, potaliya, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.100:2.8: Imesaṁ kho, potaliya, catunnaṁ puggalānaṁ katamo te puggalo khamati abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā”ti?
root_text.an4.100:3.1: “Cattārome, bho gotama, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.100:3.3: Idha, bho gotama, ekacco puggalo avaṇṇārahassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena, no ca kho vaṇṇārahassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena.
root_text.an4.100:3.4: Idha pana, bho gotama, ekacco puggalo vaṇṇārahassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena, no ca kho avaṇṇārahassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena.
root_text.an4.100:3.5: Idha pana, bho gotama, ekacco puggalo neva avaṇṇārahassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena, no ca vaṇṇārahassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena.
root_text.an4.100:3.6: Idha pana, bho gotama, ekacco puggalo avaṇṇārahassa ca avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena, vaṇṇārahassa ca vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena.
root_text.an4.100:3.7: Ime kho, bho gotama, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.100:3.8: Imesaṁ, bho gotama, catunnaṁ puggalānaṁ yvāyaṁ puggalo neva avaṇṇārahassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena, no ca vaṇṇārahassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena;
root_text.an4.100:3.9: ayaṁ me puggalo khamati imesaṁ catunnaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
root_text.an4.100:3.11: Abhikkantā hesā, bho gotama, yadidaṁ upekkhā”ti.
root_text.an4.100:4.2: Katame cattāro …pe… ime kho, potaliya, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.100:4.3: Imesaṁ kho, potaliya, catunnaṁ puggalānaṁ yvāyaṁ puggalo avaṇṇārahassa ca avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena, vaṇṇārahassa ca vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena;
root_text.an4.100:4.4: ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
root_text.an4.100:4.6: Abhikkantā hesā, potaliya, yadidaṁ tattha tattha kālaññutā”ti.
root_text.an4.100:5.1: “Cattārome, bho gotama, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.100:5.2: Katame cattāro …pe… ime kho, bho gotama, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.100:5.3: Imesaṁ, bho gotama, catunnaṁ puggalānaṁ yvāyaṁ puggalo avaṇṇārahassa ca avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena, vaṇṇārahassa ca vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ kālena;
root_text.an4.100:5.4: ayaṁ me puggalo khamati imesaṁ catunnaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
root_text.an4.100:5.6: Abhikkantā hesā, bho gotama, yadidaṁ tattha tattha kālaññutā.
root_text.an4.100:6.1: Abhikkantaṁ, bho gotama, abhikkantaṁ, bho gotama.
root_text.an4.100:6.2: Seyyathāpi, bho gotama, nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya, paṭicchannaṁ vā vivareyya, mūḷhassa vā maggaṁ ācikkheyya, andhakāre vā telapajjotaṁ dhāreyya: ‘cakkhumanto rūpāni dakkhantī’ti; evamevaṁ bhotā gotamena anekapariyāyena dhammo pakāsito.
root_text.an4.100:6.3: Esāhaṁ bhavantaṁ gotamaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi dhammañca bhikkhusaṅghañca.
root_text.an4.100:6.4: Upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.an4.100:1.1: Then the wanderer Potaliya went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an4.100:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side, and the Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an4.100:2.1: “Potaliya, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.100:2.3: One individual criticizes those deserving of criticism at the right time, truthfully and correctly. But they don’t praise those deserving of praise at the right time, truthfully and correctly.
translation_text.an4.100:2.4: Another individual praises those deserving of praise … But they don’t criticize those deserving of criticism …
translation_text.an4.100:2.5: Another individual doesn’t praise those deserving of praise … Nor do they criticize those deserving of criticism …
translation_text.an4.100:2.6: Another individual criticizes those deserving of criticism at the right time, truthfully and correctly. And they praise those deserving of praise at the right time, truthfully and correctly.
translation_text.an4.100:2.7: These are the four individuals found in the world.
translation_text.an4.100:2.8: Of these four individuals, who do you believe to be the finest?”
translation_text.an4.100:3.8: “Mister Gotama, of these four individuals, it is the individual who neither praises those deserving of praise at the right time, truthfully and correctly; nor criticizes those deserving of criticism at the right time, truthfully and correctly.
translation_text.an4.100:3.9: That is the individual I believe to be the finest.
translation_text.an4.100:3.11: Because, worthy Gotama, equanimity is the best.”
translation_text.an4.100:4.3: “Potaliya, of these four individuals, it is the individual who criticizes those deserving of criticism at the right time, truthfully and correctly; and praises those deserving of praise at the right time, truthfully and correctly.
translation_text.an4.100:4.4: That is the individual I consider to be the finest.
translation_text.an4.100:4.6: Because, Potaliya, understanding of time and context is the best.”
translation_text.an4.100:5.3: “Mister Gotama, of these four individuals, it is the individual who criticizes those deserving of criticism at the right time, truthfully and correctly; and praises those deserving of praise at the right time, truthfully and correctly.
translation_text.an4.100:5.4: That is the individual I believe to be the finest.
translation_text.an4.100:5.6: Because, worthy Gotama, understanding of time and context is the best.
translation_text.an4.100:6.2: As if he were righting the overturned, or revealing the hidden, or pointing out the path to the lost, or lighting a lamp in the dark so people with clear eyes can see what’s there, worthy Gotama has made the teaching clear in many ways.
translation_text.an4.100:6.3: I go for refuge to the worthy Gotama, to the teaching, and to the mendicant Saṅgha.
translation_text.an4.100:6.4: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
